comments.author: @reyquinones6553
comments.text: I'm from Vieques. The best time to enjoy it is in the winter time. Go there in the summertime, and you will think you traveled to the Sahara. Because when the temperature says 80, it means 95. From 12pm to 4 pm, most natives are inside their air-conditioned houses. Or in their hotels unless they're at the beaches. The florecent bay's best time is at a full moon. The food is good. Want breakfast, better buy your own to cook it. Weekends are party time. You get to Vieques by boat or plane, and there is a ferry boat for cars. Is a place for retirees and a peaceful life. Beaches all over the island, all of them beautiful. The people are very nice. Speaking English too. Carefull with the ladies, their men are very jealous. Seafood is great, but a bit expensive for tourists. But you can always find a fisherman. The nights are cool in winter, very hot in the summer. But the skies are so beautiful at night, even the moon looks like you can reach out and touch it. In the rainy season, the mosquitoes are merciless. Ho, and if you hear a hurricane is coming, get out fast, unless you're staying in a cement house or hotel far from the beach. If you get stuck on the island, knock on someone's door, and they'll give you shelter until it passes. And the Esperanza town is the best near the beach to go to. You can also look at the posts of it in facebook. Other than that, you will have a great time. I promise. I visit my family every two years. The only thing keeping me away is my responsibilities here in NYC. BUT WITH THE NEW COMMUNIST Mayor, I might just stay the next time I go home. Culebra is the same as Vieques but smaller.
